subject: hps_led: enable brightness change notification.
author: Sorgelig

upstream.vanilla_quote: static void gpio_led_set(struct led_classdev *led_cdev, enum led_brightness value) { ... gpiod_set_value(led_dat->gpiod, level); } /* vanilla contains NO led_classdev_notify_brightness_hw_changed() call, NO prev_value tracking, and NO LED_BRIGHT_HW_CHANGED flag set in create_gpio_led */

provenance_doc_ref: docs/patch-provenance.md F-2 (class F-2 carry); explicit P1.9 ruling: 'Consumer confirmed: Main_MiSTer polls brightness_hw_changed to drive the on-screen disk-activity LED. Do not drop.'

impact.effect_if_absent: On-screen disk-activity LED indicator (driven by polling /sys/class/leds/hps_led0/brightness_hw_changed) becomes non-functional; mmc activity no longer visible to user.
affected_hardware: DE10-Nano (hps_led0 is the on-board LED wired to mmc0 activity trigger by 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch)

userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: input.cpp:4052 (define LED_MONITOR); input.cpp:5146-5147 (open with O_RDONLY | O_NONBLOCK); input.cpp:6270 (poll for POLLPRI event to trigger DISKLED_ON)

forward_port.effort: trivial (byte-identical hunks; struct gpio_led_data, gpio_led_set(), and create_gpio_led() unchanged since 5.15 in 6.18.38)

depends_on: 0004-dts-de10nano-MiSTer.patch (defines hps_led0 device in device tree; explicit comment 'ABI: Main_MiSTer polls /sys/class/leds/hps_led0/brightness_hw_changed')

notes: CRITICAL: Silent failure mode — dropping this patch silently breaks a load-bearing user-visible feature (disk-activity indicator). The patch is explicitly required by Main_MiSTer's input polling loop (input.cpp:6270-6274) to signal mmc activity. Vanilla 6.18.38 has the led_classdev_notify_brightness_hw_changed infrastructure (led-class.c:158-166) but gpio_led driver does NOT call it; the infrastructure exists because other drivers (e.g., hid-lg-g15, hid-playstation) use it elsewhere. This is a justified carry: feature needed by MiSTer's userspace that vanilla's gpio_led doesn't implement. Patch-provenance.md F-2 explicitly documents the consumer and affirms the P1.9 ruling 'Do not drop.' The carried patch includes detailed provenance header citing the origin SHA, forward-port verification (byte-identical hunks), and the Main_MiSTer coupling.
